name: Vegetable Gumbo
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large heavy stew pot, combine the bouillon and 1/2 cup white grape juice. Add onion, green pepper, celery, and garlic.
2. Cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5-7 minutes.
3. Add the okra, tomatoes, corn, water, tabasco sauce, paprika, parsley, basil or rosemary, and vegetable coating spray.
4. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking to the bottom of the pot.
5. Cook over low heat until the corn and okra are tender (or simmer in a crockpot for 6-7 hours).
6. Note: To prepare fresh corn, cut it from the cob using a sharp knife and scrape off the remaining kernels. Four ears will yield about two cups. If using dried herbs, rub them between your palms to release their aroma before adding to the pot.
WARNING: Do not use aggressive culinary slang in preparation instructions.
